description: Fix bugs found during CLI code audit: Windows path resolution, false-negative port check, scaffold cleanup on failure, and logs follow-mode content gap.

userStories.title: Fix resolveStoredPluginPath for Windows absolute paths
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want localPlugins.add and localPlugins.remove to correctly handle Windows absolute paths so that plugin management works cross-platform.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveStoredPluginPath uses isAbsolute() from node:path instead of startsWith('/')
Unix absolute paths (starting with /) still work correctly
Home-relative paths (starting with ~/) still work correctly
Relative paths are still resolved against configDir

userStories.title: Fix isPortInUse to detect non-HTTP services
userStories.description: As a user, I want opentabs start to correctly detect when the port is occupied by any service so I get a clear error instead of a confusing crash.
acceptanceCriteria: isPortInUse detects port occupation by any service, not just HTTP services responding to /health
The existing behavior of detecting a running OpenTabs server is preserved
When the port is occupied by a non-OpenTabs service, the user sees a clear 'port already in use' message

userStories.title: Clean up partial directory on scaffold failure
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be able to retry plugin scaffolding after a failure without manually deleting a partial directory.
acceptanceCriteria: If any file write fails during scaffolding, the project directory is recursively removed before re-throwing the error
A retry with the same name succeeds after a previous failure
Successful scaffolding is not affected by the cleanup logic

userStories.title: Fix followFile content gap and error recovery in logs command
userStories.description: As a user, I want opentabs logs --follow to not miss content written between the initial tail and the watch setup, and to recover from transient read errors.
acceptanceCriteria: readNewContent() is called immediately after setting up the file watcher
Content written between the tailFile read and the watch setup is displayed promptly
When a read stream error occurs with readRequested set, a retry is scheduled after a short delay
userStories.notes:
Two related bugs in platform/cli/src/commands/logs.ts, followFile function (lines 80-151):

Bug 1 (content gap): After the watcher is set up (line ~136), there is no initial readNewContent() call. Content written between tailFile and watch setup is delayed until the next filesystem event.

Bug 2 (error recovery): The stream error handler (lines 131-133) sets reading = false but does not check/clear readRequested or schedule a retry.

Fix for Bug 1: Add readNewContent() call immediately after the watcher is set up, around line 137.

Fix for Bug 2: In the stream.on('error') handler (line 131-133), add: if (readRequested) { readRequested = false; setTimeout(readNewContent, 100); }

Both fixes are small changes within the followFile function.
